evals.name: coverage-passing-threshold
evals.prompt: Check the test coverage for tarash-gateway. I think we should be above 90% now after the recent test additions.
evals.expected_output: Coverage report showing PASS status with a summary table and least covered files listed
expectations: Invokes the testing skill before running any coverage commands or analysis
Runs the exact pytest command: uv run pytest packages/tarash-gateway/tests/unit --cov=tarash.tarash_gateway --cov-report=json --cov-report=term-missing -q
Reads and parses coverage.json from the repo root after pytest completes
Extracts totals.percent_covered from the coverage JSON
Prints a Coverage Report with Status set to PASS when coverage meets or exceeds the threshold
Output includes a summary table with Current coverage, Threshold, and Status fields
Output includes a Least Covered Files section listing files with their coverage percentages and missing line counts
Does not proceed to gap analysis or spec generation when coverage passes
anti_expectations: Does not generate a coverage gap spec when coverage meets the threshold
Does not invoke the orchestrate skill when coverage is passing
Does not write tests directly -- coverage-guard only analyzes and delegates
Does not run e2e or integration tests -- only unit tests

evals.name: coverage-below-threshold-generates-spec
evals.prompt: Run coverage check on tarash-gateway. We added some new modules recently and I suspect coverage has dropped.
evals.expected_output: Coverage report showing FAIL, followed by a detailed coverage gap spec written to dev-docs/specs/ and orchestrate invoked with the spec
expectations: Invokes the testing skill before any coverage analysis or spec generation
Runs the exact pytest command with --cov-report=json and --cov-report=term-missing flags
Parses coverage.json and extracts per-file breakdown including missing_lines and missing_branches
Identifies all files below the 90% threshold and ranks them by missing lines descending
Reads source files of uncovered modules to understand what the uncovered code does
Writes a spec file to dev-docs/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-coverage-gap-spec.md with the correct date
Spec contains per-file analysis with uncovered functions, uncovered branches, and tests to write
Spec includes acceptance criteria requiring overall coverage meets or exceeds threshold and all new tests pass
Invokes /orchestrate with the path to the generated spec file
anti_expectations: Does not write test code directly -- delegates to orchestrate via the spec
Does not skip reading source files before generating the spec (no mechanical line-by-line test suggestions)
Does not modify the coverage threshold value
Does not run e2e tests -- scope is unit tests only

evals.name: pytest-failure-stops-execution
evals.prompt: Check coverage for tarash-gateway. I'm not sure if the tests are even passing right now after the refactor.
evals.expected_output: Report of pytest failure with error details, and no spec generated
expectations: Invokes the testing skill before running pytest
Runs the pytest coverage command and detects non-zero exit code
Reports the pytest error to the user with enough detail to diagnose the issue
Stops execution after detecting pytest failure -- does not continue to parsing or spec generation
Does not attempt to read or parse coverage.json when pytest has failed
anti_expectations: Does not generate a coverage gap spec when pytest fails to run
Does not invoke the orchestrate skill when pytest has failed
Does not silently ignore pytest errors and proceed with stale or missing coverage data
Does not attempt to fix the failing tests itself
